input.prompt:
I just upgraded my .NET MAUI app from .NET 9 to .NET 10. On iOS, my content
is now going behind the status bar and the Dynamic Island notch area. In .NET 9
I was using `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` and it worked fine. What's the correct
way to handle this in .NET 10? Show me the XAML.

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
I just upgraded my .NET MAUI app from .NET 9 to .NET 10. On iOS, my content
is now going behind the status bar and the Dynamic Island notch area. In .NET 9
I was using `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` and it worked fine. What's the correct
way to handle this in .NET 10? Show me the XAML.

data.content:
In .NET 10, the iOS-specific `UseSafeArea` attached property was **removed**. It's replaced by the cross-platform `SafeAreaRegions` property on `Page`:

```xml
<!-- .NET 9 (old) -->
<ContentPage xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;assembly=Microsoft.Maui.Controls"
             ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True">

<!-- .NET 10 (new) -->
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             SafeAreaRegions="All">
```

`SafeAreaRegions` is a flags enum — valid values are `None`, `Left`, `Top`, `Right`, `Bottom`, and `All`. You can combine them:

```xml
SafeAreaRegions="Top,Bottom"
```

No platform-specific XML namespace needed anymore.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI migration issue from .NET 9 to .NET 10 regarding safe area handling on iOS. This is a known breaking change in .NET 10 MAUI.

In .NET 10, the `ios:Page.UseSafeArea` attached property was removed. The replacement is to use `SafeAreaRegions` property directly on the page.

The new approach in .NET 10 is:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             SafeAreaRegions="All">
```

The `SafeAreaRegions` enum has values like `All`, `None`, `Left`, `Top`, `Right`, `Bottom`, etc.


Now I'm preparing the updated XAML example to show how to properly configure the safe area regions for the migrated page.
